dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - April 14, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**Diamondbacks Douse Orioles in Baltimore, 4-3**

The Arizona Diamondbacks took down the Baltimore Orioles in a thrilling 4-3 victory. Ildemaro Vargas went yard twice, driving in three runs, including a go-ahead three-run homer in the top of the 8th. Merrill Kelly earned the win, while Trevor Rogers suffered the loss. The Diamondbacks' bullpen was solid, with Paul Sewald securing the save.

**Nationals Edge Pirates in Pittsburgh, 5-4**

The Washington Nationals outlasted the Pittsburgh Pirates in a back-and-forth matchup, 5-4. CJ Abrams homered and drove in two runs, while Mitchell Parker tossed two scoreless innings to earn the win. Joey Bart's solo homer in the 7th proved to be the difference for the Nationals.

**Trout and Angels Crush Yankees, 7-1**

Mike Trout and the Los Angeles Angels dominated the New York Yankees, 7-1. Trout didn't homer, but drove in a run and scored twice. Reid Detmers allowed just one earned run over seven innings, striking out nine to earn the win. Yoán Moncada homered and drove in three runs for the Angels.

**Twins Rout Red Sox, 6-0**

Byron Buxton went yard twice, driving in two runs, to lead the Minnesota Twins to a 6-0 shutout of the Boston Red Sox. Mick Abel allowed just four hits over seven scoreless innings, striking out ten to earn the win.

**Rays Outlast White Sox, 8-5**

Everson Pereira homered and drove in three runs to help the Tampa Bay Rays outlast the Chicago White Sox, 8-5. Shane McClanahan allowed two earned runs over five innings to earn the win.

**Blue Jays Edge Brewers, 9-7**

Jake Bauers homered and drove in three runs to lead the Toronto Blue Jays to a 9-7 comeback win over the Milwaukee Brewers. Louis Varland earned the win in relief, while the Blue Jays' bullpen held off the Brewers in the late innings.

**Cardinals Top Guardians, 6-5**

JJ Wetherholt homered twice, driving in three runs, to lead the St. Louis Cardinals to a 6-5 win over the Cleveland Guardians. Riley O'Brien earned the win in relief.

**Astros Edge Rockies, 7-6**

Hunter Goodman homered twice, driving in two runs, to help the Houston Astros outlast the Colorado Rockies, 7-6. AJ Blubaugh earned the win in relief, while Enyel De Los Santos secured the save.

**Padres Shut Down Mariners, 4-1**

Xander Bogaerts drove in three runs to lead the San Diego Padres to a 4-1 win over the Seattle Mariners. Michael King earned the win, allowing just one earned run over six innings.

**Athletics Edge Rangers, 2-1**

Jeffrey Springs allowed just one earned run over six and two-thirds innings to lead the Oakland Athletics to a 2-1 win over the Texas Rangers. Jacob Wilson drove in two runs for the Athletics.

**Dodgers Edge Mets, 2-1**

Nolan McLean struck out eight over seven innings to lead the Los Angeles Dodgers to a 2-1 win over the New York Mets. Francisco Lindor homered for the Mets.
